title: Particles and Quantum Phenomena
description: Constituents of the atom, the standard model of quarks and leptons, the four fundamental forces, the photoelectric effect and wave-particle duality.
objectives: Describe the constituents of the atom and classify particles as quarks (up, down) or leptons (electron, electron neutrino)
State the four fundamental forces and their approximate relative strengths and ranges
Apply conservation of charge, baryon number and lepton number to particle interactions and decays
Explain the photoelectric effect and apply Einstein's photoelectric equation hf = Φ + KE_max
Calculate the de Broglie wavelength of a particle from its momentum and describe evidence for wave-particle duality
Relate discrete atomic energy levels to photon emission/absorption and line spectra using E = hf
